claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. His name is Alex, and he's 8 years old. He lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me ask a few more questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Are you responsible for Alex's care? In other words, do you provide for his needs such as food, clothes, and general upkeep?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm fully responsible for Alex. I take care of all his needs - his food, clothes, everything he needs day to day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Just a couple more questions:

Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect. One final question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex? (Remember, only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16), which meets the age requirement
- You live with Alex in the UK
- You are fully responsible for his care and provide for all his needs
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Since you meet all these criteria, you should be able to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

If you haven't already claimed, you can proceed with making your application. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK and is fully responsible for his care, providing for all his needs including food and clothes. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is fully responsible for their 8-year-old son Alex, lives with him in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is fully responsible for their 8-year-old son Alex, lives with him in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.
